revision.text:
'''鬼神'''（きじん、きしん、おにがみ）は、「きじん」または「きしん」と音読みした場合の第一義としては天地万物の[[霊魂]]あるいは[[神々]]を意味し、「おにがみ」と訓読みした場合は目に見えない精霊または荒々しく恐ろしい神を意味する<ref>「[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%AC%BC%E7%A5%9E-50543#E7.B2.BE.E9.81.B8.E7.89.88.20.E6.97.A5.E6.9C.AC.E5.9B.BD.E8.AA.9E.E5.A4.A7.E8.BE.9E.E5.85.B8 鬼神]」『精選版 [[日本国語大辞典]]』[[小学館]]、[[コトバンク]]。2021年7月16日閲覧。</ref>。
== 中国 == {{中華圏の事物 | ピン音 = guǐshén | カタカナ = クウィシェン | ひらがな = きしん}} [[中国]]における鬼神とは対照的な2つの[[霊]]である鬼と神を指す。
鬼（き）は生者に禍（わざわい）をもたらす霊であり、[[悪鬼]]として顕現し、祈祓（きふつ、いのりはらい）の対象である<ref name="麦谷邦夫" />。鬼は生者が持つ魄（はく）の死後の姿であり、魄は生者の内にあって肉体をつかさどる陰気の霊であり、その魄が死後に地上にとどまって鬼となる<ref name="麦谷邦夫" />。その死が[[横死]]であったり、身寄りがなく死後に祀（まつ）る者のない場合に悪鬼・幽鬼として生者に祟（たた）る<ref name="渡邊昭五">[[渡邊昭五]]「[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%AC%BC%28%E3%81%8D%29-1522360 鬼（き）]」『[[日本大百科全書]](ニッポニカ)』[[小学館]]、コトバンク。2021年7月23日閲覧。</ref>。
神（しん）は生者に福をもたらす霊であり、善神として顕現し、[[祭祀]]の対象である<ref name="麦谷邦夫" />。神は生者が持つ[[魂]]の死後の姿であり、魂は生者の内にあって精神をつかさどる陽気の霊であり、その魂が天上に昇って神・[[神霊]]となる<ref name="麦谷邦夫">「[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%AC%BC%E7%A5%9E-50543#E4.B8.96.E7.95.8C.E5.A4.A7.E7.99.BE.E7.A7.91.E4.BA.8B.E5.85.B8.20.E7.AC.AC.EF.BC.92.E7.89.88 鬼神]」『[[世界大百科事典]]』第2版、[[平凡社]]、[[コトバンク]]。2021年7月23日閲覧。</ref><ref name="渡邊昭五" />。
また、鬼神は天地のあらゆる物の創造・変化・消滅にかかる人知の及ばぬ霊妙な働きを指す場合がある<ref name="麦谷邦夫" />。
== 朝鮮 == {{朝鮮の事物 | hangeul = 귀신 | katakana = クィシン}} [[朝鮮半島]]の[[民間信仰]]における鬼神は、生者あるいは死者の内にある[[霊]]とは異なり、宿る対象を持たず、何にも従属しない孤立した存在である<ref name="GWE鬼神" /><ref name="靈魂崇拜" />。この点で鬼神は人に宿る霊や人以外に宿る[[精]]（精霊）あるいは[[神明]]（神々）と区別されるが、宿る対象についての観念がはっきりしなくなると霊や精あるいは神明と区別されなくなる<ref name="GWE鬼神" /><ref name="靈魂崇拜">「[https://ko.wikisource.org/wiki/%EA%B8%80%EB%A1%9C%EB%B2%8C_%EC%84%B8%EA%B3%84_%EB%8C%80%EB%B0%B1%EA%B3%BC%EC%82%AC%EC%A0%84/%EC%A2%85%EA%B5%90%C2%B7%EC%B2%A0%ED%95%99/%ED%95%9C%EA%B5%AD%EC%9D%98_%EC%A2%85%EA%B5%90/%EB%AF%BC_%EA%B0%84_%EC%8B%A0_%EC%95%99/%EC%88%AD_%EB%B0%B0#%EC%98%81%ED%98%BC%EC%88%AD%EB%B0%B0 靈魂崇拜]」『グローバル世界大百科事典』ウィキソース。2021年7月16日閲覧。</ref>。
鬼神は腰より下が[[障子紙]]の服で、足はやせ細りまるで[[にかわ]]のようで骨だけ残った姿をしていて、まもなく病を得て死ぬ身の上であり、神明の前で立ちすくんでしまう力弱い存在で、力の強い人に睨まれるとだんだん小さくなって消えうせる存在である<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。人が与える食べ物におとなしくして話を聞く見えない存在だが、鬼神のなかには意地悪をするものがある<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。
鬼神は昼間はあちこち空中でふらふらするが、夜はじめじめした所を探して体を休めることもする<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。だいたい古木が鬼神の住みかになる<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。また人家を訪れるときがあるが、そのとき人は鬼神が嫌う方法を使って入って来ないように防いで、[[塩]]を撒いたり[[豆]]を撒いたりする<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。鬼神の住みかは藪の中や土の中、池、井戸端などじめじめした所などで、どこでも出入りすることが出来る<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。鬼神はいったん人の家に入ると食べ物を提供されてそこを去る。白昼に石を投げる乱暴を働いたり、時に猟犬の吠える声を出したり口笛を吹いたりして強風を起こしたりする<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。夜には道を通る人を怖がらせて火遊びもする<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。この火を鬼火（[[トッケビ]]の火）とも言い人々が怖がる<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。
鬼神は時に賢く、一国や一家族の滅亡を予言したり警告したり、あるいは遺失物の在りかを知らせたりもする<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。しかしだいたい鬼神は前述のごとく、より勢力があるものに追われる身なのである<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。[[疫病神]]を追い払ったという説話で知られる{{仮リンク|処容|ko|처용}}のような強い人物を描いたお守り（符籍）の前では立ちすくんでしまい、追い払われる存在である<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。また鬼神のうちで強い鬼神は弱い鬼神を殺害したりする<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。なお神明も泊まる居場所がなかったり、生存者から供え物をもらえないと鬼神になる場合がある<ref name="GWE鬼神">「[https://ko.wikisource.org/wiki/%EA%B8%80%EB%A1%9C%EB%B2%8C_%EC%84%B8%EA%B3%84_%EB%8C%80%EB%B0%B1%EA%B3%BC%EC%82%AC%EC%A0%84/%EC%A2%85%EA%B5%90%C2%B7%EC%B2%A0%ED%95%99/%ED%95%9C%EA%B5%AD%EC%9D%98_%EC%A2%85%EA%B5%90/%EB%AF%BC_%EA%B0%84_%EC%8B%A0_%EC%95%99/%EC%88%AD_%EB%B0%B0#%EA%B7%80%EC%8B%A0 鬼神]」『グローバル世界大百科事典』ウィキソース。2021年7月16日閲覧。</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *{{グローバル百科}}
== 関連項目 == *[[新井白石]]『[[鬼神論]]』 *[[山片蟠桃#思想]]『[[夢の代]]』 *[[平田篤胤]]『[[鬼神新論]]』
{{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きしん}} [[Category:鬼]]

また、鬼神は天地のあらゆる物の創造・変化・消滅にかかる人知の及ばぬ霊妙な働きを指す場合がある<ref name="麦谷邦夫" />。
== 朝鮮 == {{朝鮮の事物 | hangeul = 귀신 | katakana = クィシン}} [[朝鮮半島]]の[[民間信仰]]における鬼神は、生者あるいは死者の内にある[[霊]]とは異なり、宿る対象を持たず、何にも従属しない孤立した存在である<ref name="GWE鬼神" /><ref name="靈魂崇拜" />。この点で鬼神は人に宿る霊や人以外に宿る[[精]]（精霊）あるいは[[神明]]（神々）と区別されるが、宿る対象についての観念がはっきりしなくなると霊や精あるいは神明と区別されなくなる<ref name="GWE鬼神" /><ref name="靈魂崇拜">「[https://ko.wikisource.org/wiki/%EA%B8%80%EB%A1%9C%EB%B2%8C_%EC%84%B8%EA%B3%84_%EB%8C%80%EB%B0%B1%EA%B3%BC%EC%82%AC%EC%A0%84/%EC%A2%85%EA%B5%90%C2%B7%EC%B2%A0%ED%95%99/%ED%95%9C%EA%B5%AD%EC%9D%98_%EC%A2%85%EA%B5%90/%EB%AF%BC_%EA%B0%84_%EC%8B%A0_%EC%95%99/%EC%88%AD_%EB%B0%B0#%EC%98%81%ED%98%BC%EC%88%AD%EB%B0%B0 靈魂崇拜]」『グローバル世界大百科事典』ウィキソース。2021年7月16日閲覧。</ref>。
鬼神は腰より下が[[障子紙]]の服で、足はやせ細りまるで[[にかわ]]のようで骨だけ残った姿をしていて、まもなく病を得て死ぬ身の上であり、神明の前で立ちすくんでしまう力弱い存在で、力の強い人に睨まれるとだんだん小さくなって消えうせる存在である<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。人が与える食べ物におとなしくして話を聞く見えない存在だが、鬼神のなかには意地悪をするものがある<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。
鬼神は昼間はあちこち空中でふらふらするが、夜はじめじめした所を探して体を休めることもする<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。だいたい古木が鬼神の住みかになる<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。また人家を訪れるときがあるが、そのとき人は鬼神が嫌う方法を使って入って来ないように防いで、[[塩]]を撒いたり[[豆]]を撒いたりする<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。鬼神の住みかは藪の中や土の中、池、井戸端などじめじめした所などで、どこでも出入りすることが出来る<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。鬼神はいったん人の家に入ると食べ物を提供されてそこを去る。白昼に石を投げる乱暴を働いたり、時に猟犬の吠える声を出したり口笛を吹いたりして強風を起こしたりする<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。夜には道を通る人を怖がらせて火遊びもする<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。この火を鬼火（[[トッケビ]]の火）とも言い人々が怖がる<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。
鬼神は時に賢く、一国や一家族の滅亡を予言したり警告したり、あるいは遺失物の在りかを知らせたりもする<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。しかしだいたい鬼神は前述のごとく、より勢力があるものに追われる身なのである<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。[[疫病神]]を追い払ったという説話で知られる{{仮リンク|処容|ko|처용}}のような強い人物を描いたお守り（符籍）の前では立ちすくんでしまい、追い払われる存在である<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。また鬼神のうちで強い鬼神は弱い鬼神を殺害したりする<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。なお神明も泊まる居場所がなかったり、生存者から供え物をもらえないと鬼神になる場合がある<ref name="GWE鬼神">「[https://ko.wikisource.org/wiki/%EA%B8%80%EB%A1%9C%EB%B2%8C_%EC%84%B8%EA%B3%84_%EB%8C%80%EB%B0%B1%EA%B3%BC%EC%82%AC%EC%A0%84/%EC%A2%85%EA%B5%90%C2%B7%EC%B2%A0%ED%95%99/%ED%95%9C%EA%B5%AD%EC%9D%98_%EC%A2%85%EA%B5%90/%EB%AF%BC_%EA%B0%84_%EC%8B%A0_%EC%95%99/%EC%88%AD_%EB%B0%B0#%EA%B7%80%EC%8B%A0 鬼神]」『グローバル世界大百科事典』ウィキソース。2021年7月16日閲覧。</ref>。

'''鬼神'''（きじん、きしん、おにがみ）は、「きじん」または「きしん」と音読みした場合の第一義としては天地万物の[[霊魂]]あるいは[[神々]]を意味し、「おにがみ」と訓読みした場合は目に見えない精霊または荒々しく恐ろしい神を意味する<ref>「[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%AC%BC%E7%A5%9E-50543#E7.B2.BE.E9.81.B8.E7.89.88.20.E6.97.A5.E6.9C.AC.E5.9B.BD.E8.AA.9E.E5.A4.A7.E8.BE.9E.E5.85.B8 鬼神]」『精選版 [[日本国語大辞典]]』[[小学館]]、[[コトバンク]]。2021年7月16日閲覧。</ref>。
== 中国 == {{中華圏の事物 | ピン音 = guǐshén | カタカナ = グイシェン | ひらがな = きしん}} [[中国]]における鬼神は対照的な2つの[[霊]]である鬼と神を指す。
鬼（き）は生者に禍（わざわい）をもたらす霊であり、[[悪鬼]]として顕現し、祈祓（きふつ、いのりはらい）の対象である<ref name="麦谷邦夫" />。鬼は生者が持つ魄（はく）の死後の姿であり、魄は生者の内にあって肉体をつかさどる陰気の霊であり、その魄が死後に地上にとどまって鬼となる<ref name="麦谷邦夫" />。その死が[[横死]]であったり、身寄りがなく死後に祀（まつ）る者のない場合に悪鬼・幽鬼として生者に祟（たた）る<ref name="渡邊昭五">[[渡邊昭五]]「[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%AC%BC%28%E3%81%8D%29-1522360 鬼（き）]」『[[日本大百科全書]](ニッポニカ)』[[小学館]]、コトバンク。2021年7月23日閲覧。</ref>。
神（しん）は生者に福をもたらす霊であり、善神として顕現し、[[祭祀]]の対象である<ref name="麦谷邦夫" />。神は生者が持つ[[魂]]の死後の姿であり、魂は生者の内にあって精神をつかさどる陽気の霊であり、その魂が天上に昇って神・[[神霊]]となる<ref name="麦谷邦夫">「[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%AC%BC%E7%A5%9E-50543#E4.B8.96.E7.95.8C.E5.A4.A7.E7.99.BE.E7.A7.91.E4.BA.8B.E5.85.B8.20.E7.AC.AC.EF.BC.92.E7.89.88 鬼神]」『[[世界大百科事典]]』第2版、[[平凡社]]、[[コトバンク]]。2021年7月23日閲覧。</ref><ref name="渡邊昭五" />。
また、鬼神は天地のあらゆる物の創造・変化・消滅にかかる人知の及ばぬ霊妙な働きを指す場合がある<ref name="麦谷邦夫" />。

revision.comment: 2月1日の版で付加された少々過剰なリンクを除去
revision.text:
'''鬼神'''（きじん、きしん、おにがみ）は、「きじん」または「きしん」と音読みした場合の第一義としては天地万物の[[霊魂]]あるいは[[神々]]を意味し、「おにがみ」と訓読みした場合は目に見えない精霊または荒々しく恐ろしい神を意味する<ref>「[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%AC%BC%E7%A5%9E-50543#E7.B2.BE.E9.81.B8.E7.89.88.20.E6.97.A5.E6.9C.AC.E5.9B.BD.E8.AA.9E.E5.A4.A7.E8.BE.9E.E5.85.B8 鬼神]」『精選版 [[日本国語大辞典]]』[[小学館]]、[[コトバンク]]。2021年7月16日閲覧。</ref>。
== 中国 == {{中華圏の事物 | ピン音 = guǐshén | カタカナ = グイシェン | ひらがな = きしん}} [[中国]]における鬼神は対照的な2つの[[霊]]である鬼と神を指す。
鬼（き）は生者に禍（わざわい）をもたらす霊であり、[[悪鬼]]として顕現し、祈祓（きふつ、いのりはらい）の対象である<ref name="麦谷邦夫" />。鬼は生者が持つ魄（はく）の死後の姿であり、魄は生者の内にあって肉体をつかさどる陰気の霊であり、その魄が死後に地上にとどまって鬼となる<ref name="麦谷邦夫" />。その死が[[横死]]であったり、身寄りがなく死後に祀（まつ）る者のない場合に悪鬼・幽鬼として生者に祟（たた）る<ref name="渡邊昭五">[[渡邊昭五]]「[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%AC%BC%28%E3%81%8D%29-1522360 鬼（き）]」『[[日本大百科全書]](ニッポニカ)』[[小学館]]、コトバンク。2021年7月23日閲覧。</ref>。
神（しん）は生者に福をもたらす霊であり、善神として顕現し、[[祭祀]]の対象である<ref name="麦谷邦夫" />。神は生者が持つ[[魂]]の死後の姿であり、魂は生者の内にあって精神をつかさどる陽気の霊であり、その魂が天上に昇って神・[[神霊]]となる<ref name="麦谷邦夫">「[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%AC%BC%E7%A5%9E-50543#E4.B8.96.E7.95.8C.E5.A4.A7.E7.99.BE.E7.A7.91.E4.BA.8B.E5.85.B8.20.E7.AC.AC.EF.BC.92.E7.89.88 鬼神]」『[[世界大百科事典]]』第2版、[[平凡社]]、[[コトバンク]]。2021年7月23日閲覧。</ref><ref name="渡邊昭五" />。
また、鬼神は天地のあらゆる物の創造・変化・消滅にかかる人知の及ばぬ霊妙な働きを指す場合がある<ref name="麦谷邦夫" />。
== 朝鮮 == {{朝鮮の事物 | hangeul = 귀신 | katakana = クィシン}} [[朝鮮半島]]の[[民間信仰]]における鬼神は、生者あるいは死者の内にある[[霊]]とは異なり、宿る対象を持たず、何にも従属しない孤立した存在である<ref name="GWE鬼神" /><ref name="靈魂崇拜" />。この点で鬼神は人に宿る霊や人以外に宿る[[精]]（精霊）あるいは{{仮リンク|神明 (朝鮮)|label=神明|ko|신명 (신령)}}と区別されるが、宿る対象についての観念がはっきりしなくなると霊や精あるいは神明と区別されなくなる<ref name="GWE鬼神" /><ref name="靈魂崇拜">「[https://ko.wikisource.org/wiki/%EA%B8%80%EB%A1%9C%EB%B2%8C_%EC%84%B8%EA%B3%84_%EB%8C%80%EB%B0%B1%EA%B3%BC%EC%82%AC%EC%A0%84/%EC%A2%85%EA%B5%90%C2%B7%EC%B2%A0%ED%95%99/%ED%95%9C%EA%B5%AD%EC%9D%98_%EC%A2%85%EA%B5%90/%EB%AF%BC_%EA%B0%84_%EC%8B%A0_%EC%95%99/%EC%88%AD_%EB%B0%B0#%EC%98%81%ED%98%BC%EC%88%AD%EB%B0%B0 靈魂崇拜]」『グローバル世界大百科事典』ウィキソース。2021年7月16日閲覧。</ref>。
鬼神は腰より下が[[障子紙]]の服で、足はやせ細りまるで[[にかわ]]のようで骨だけ残った姿をしていて、まもなく病を得て死ぬ身の上であり、神明の前で立ちすくんでしまう力弱い存在で、力の強い人に睨まれるとだんだん小さくなって消えうせる存在である<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。人が与える食べ物におとなしくして話を聞く見えない存在だが、鬼神のなかには意地悪をするものがある<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。
鬼神は昼間はあちこち空中でふらふらするが、夜はじめじめした所を探して体を休めることもする<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。だいたい古木が鬼神の住みかになる<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。また人家を訪れるときがあるが、そのとき人は鬼神が嫌う方法を使って入って来ないように防いで、[[塩]]を撒いたり[[豆]]を撒いたりする<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。鬼神の住みかは藪の中や土の中、池、井戸端などじめじめした所などで、どこでも出入りすることが出来る<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。鬼神はいったん人の家に入ると食べ物を提供されてそこを去る。白昼に石を投げる乱暴を働いたり、時に猟犬の吠える声を出したり口笛を吹いたりして強風を起こしたりする<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。夜には道を通る人を怖がらせて火遊びもする<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。この火を鬼火（[[トッケビ]]の火）とも言い人々が怖がる<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。
鬼神は時に賢く、一国や一家族の滅亡を予言したり警告したり、あるいは遺失物の在りかを知らせたりもする<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。しかしだいたい鬼神は前述のごとく、より勢力があるものに追われる身なのである<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。[[疫病神]]を追い払ったという説話で知られる{{仮リンク|処容|ko|처용}}のような強い人物を描いたお守り（符籍）の前では立ちすくんでしまい、追い払われる存在である<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。また鬼神のうちで強い鬼神は弱い鬼神を殺害したりする<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。なお神明も泊まる居場所がなかったり、生存者から供え物をもらえないと鬼神になる場合がある<ref name="GWE鬼神">「[https://ko.wikisource.org/wiki/%EA%B8%80%EB%A1%9C%EB%B2%8C_%EC%84%B8%EA%B3%84_%EB%8C%80%EB%B0%B1%EA%B3%BC%EC%82%AC%EC%A0%84/%EC%A2%85%EA%B5%90%C2%B7%EC%B2%A0%ED%95%99/%ED%95%9C%EA%B5%AD%EC%9D%98_%EC%A2%85%EA%B5%90/%EB%AF%BC_%EA%B0%84_%EC%8B%A0_%EC%95%99/%EC%88%AD_%EB%B0%B0#%EA%B7%80%EC%8B%A0 鬼神]」『グローバル世界大百科事典』ウィキソース。2021年7月16日閲覧。</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *{{グローバル百科}}
== 関連項目 == *[[新井白石]]『[[鬼神論]]』 *[[山片蟠桃#思想]]『[[夢の代]]』 *[[平田篤胤]]『[[鬼神新論]]』
{{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きしん}} [[Category:鬼]]

神（しん）は生者に福をもたらす霊であり、善神として顕現し、[[祭祀]]の対象である<ref name="麦谷邦夫" />。神は生者が持つ[[魂]]の死後の姿であり、魂は生者の内にあって精神をつかさどる陽気の霊であり、その魂が天上に昇って神・[[神霊]]となる<ref name="麦谷邦夫">「[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%AC%BC%E7%A5%9E-50543#E4.B8.96.E7.95.8C.E5.A4.A7.E7.99.BE.E7.A7.91.E4.BA.8B.E5.85.B8.20.E7.AC.AC.EF.BC.92.E7.89.88 鬼神]」『[[世界大百科事典]]』第2版、[[平凡社]]、[[コトバンク]]。2021年7月23日閲覧。</ref><ref name="渡邊昭五" />。
また、鬼神は天地のあらゆる物の創造・変化・消滅にかかる人知の及ばぬ霊妙な働きを指す場合がある<ref name="麦谷邦夫" />。
== 朝鮮 == {{朝鮮の事物 | hangeul = 귀신 | katakana = クィシン}} [[朝鮮半島]]の[[民間信仰]]における鬼神は、生者あるいは死者の内にある[[霊]]とは異なり、宿る対象を持たず、何にも従属しない孤立した存在である<ref name="GWE鬼神" /><ref name="靈魂崇拜" />。この点で鬼神は人に宿る霊や人以外に宿る[[精]]（精霊）あるいは{{仮リンク|神明 (朝鮮)|label=神明|ko|신명 (신령)}}と区別されるが、宿る対象についての観念がはっきりしなくなると霊や精あるいは神明と区別されなくなる<ref name="GWE鬼神" /><ref name="靈魂崇拜">「[https://ko.wikisource.org/wiki/%EA%B8%80%EB%A1%9C%EB%B2%8C_%EC%84%B8%EA%B3%84_%EB%8C%80%EB%B0%B1%EA%B3%BC%EC%82%AC%EC%A0%84/%EC%A2%85%EA%B5%90%C2%B7%EC%B2%A0%ED%95%99/%ED%95%9C%EA%B5%AD%EC%9D%98_%EC%A2%85%EA%B5%90/%EB%AF%BC_%EA%B0%84_%EC%8B%A0_%EC%95%99/%EC%88%AD_%EB%B0%B0#%EC%98%81%ED%98%BC%EC%88%AD%EB%B0%B0 靈魂崇拜]」『グローバル世界大百科事典』ウィキソース。2021年7月16日閲覧。</ref>。
鬼神は腰より下が[[障子紙]]の服で、足はやせ細りまるで[[にかわ]]のようで骨だけ残った姿をしていて、まもなく病を得て死ぬ身の上であり、神明の前で立ちすくんでしまう力弱い存在で、力の強い人に睨まれるとだんだん小さくなって消えうせる存在である<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。人が与える食べ物におとなしくして話を聞く見えない存在だが、鬼神のなかには意地悪をするものがある<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。
鬼神は昼間はあちこち空中でふらふらするが、夜はじめじめした所を探して体を休めることもする<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。だいたい古木が鬼神の住みかになる<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。また人家を訪れるときがあるが、そのとき人は鬼神が嫌う方法を使って入って来ないように防いで、[[塩]]を撒いたり[[豆]]を撒いたりする<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。鬼神の住みかは藪の中や土の中、池、井戸端などじめじめした所などで、どこでも出入りすることが出来る<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。鬼神はいったん人の家に入ると食べ物を提供されてそこを去る。白昼に石を投げる乱暴を働いたり、時に猟犬の吠える声を出したり口笛を吹いたりして強風を起こしたりする<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。夜には道を通る人を怖がらせて火遊びもする<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。この火を鬼火（[[トッケビ]]の火）とも言い人々が怖がる<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。
鬼神は時に賢く、一国や一家族の滅亡を予言したり警告したり、あるいは遺失物の在りかを知らせたりもする<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。しかしだいたい鬼神は前述のごとく、より勢力があるものに追われる身なのである<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。[[疫病神]]を追い払ったという説話で知られる{{仮リンク|処容|ko|처용}}のような強い人物を描いたお守り（符籍）の前では立ちすくんでしまい、追い払われる存在である<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。また鬼神のうちで強い鬼神は弱い鬼神を殺害したりする<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。なお神明も泊まる居場所がなかったり、生存者から供え物をもらえないと鬼神になる場合がある<ref name="GWE鬼神">「[https://ko.wikisource.org/wiki/%EA%B8%80%EB%A1%9C%EB%B2%8C_%EC%84%B8%EA%B3%84_%EB%8C%80%EB%B0%B1%EA%B3%BC%EC%82%AC%EC%A0%84/%EC%A2%85%EA%B5%90%C2%B7%EC%B2%A0%ED%95%99/%ED%95%9C%EA%B5%AD%EC%9D%98_%EC%A2%85%EA%B5%90/%EB%AF%BC_%EA%B0%84_%EC%8B%A0_%EC%95%99/%EC%88%AD_%EB%B0%B0#%EA%B7%80%EC%8B%A0 鬼神]」『グローバル世界大百科事典』ウィキソース。2021年7月16日閲覧。</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *{{グローバル百科}}
== 関連項目 == *[[新井白石]]『[[鬼神論]]』 *[[山片蟠桃#思想]]『[[夢の代]]』 *[[平田篤胤]]『[[鬼神新論]]』
{{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きしん}} [[Category:鬼]]

revision.text:
== Sanki Daigongen == '''Sanki Daigongen''' (三鬼大権現) is a {{ill|demon deity|ja|鬼神}} revered in {{ill|Tengu|ja|天狗}} worship and {{ill|mountain Buddhism|ja|山岳仏教}}, particularly within the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗}} tradition. It is also known as '''Itsukushima Sanki-bō''' (厳島三鬼坊).
== Overview == Sanki Daigongen is primarily enshrined at the {{ill|Sanki-dō|ja|三鬼堂}} on {{ill|Mount Misen|ja|弥山 (広島県)}} and within the {{ill|Maniden|ja|魔尼殿}} at {{ill|Daishō-in|ja|大聖院 (宮島)}} on {{ill|Miyajima|ja|宮島}}, located in {{ill|Hatsukaichi|ja|廿日市市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}. Sanki Daigongen is believed to command both great and small Tengu as retainers, using vast supernatural powers to save sentient beings. Locally, it is affectionately referred to as '''Sanki-san'''. It is said that {{ill|Itō Hirobumi|ja|伊藤博文}}, Japan’s first Prime Minister, was a devout follower.
The three deities of Sanki Daigongen are: * **Tsuichō Kishin (追帳鬼神)**: A deity governing the virtue of "fortune and virtue" (福徳), with {{ill|Dainichi Nyorai|ja|大日如来}} as the {{ill|honji-butsu|ja|本地仏}} (original Buddha). * **Jibi Kishin (時眉鬼神)**: A deity overseeing the virtue of "wisdom" (知恵), with {{ill|Kokūzō Bosatsu|ja|虚空蔵菩薩}} as the honji-butsu. Kokūzō Bosatsu is also the principal deity enshrined at {{ill|Misen Main Hall|ja|弥山本堂}}. * **Mara Kishin (魔羅鬼神)**: A deity associated with the virtue of "subjugation" (降伏), with {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}} as the honji-butsu.
Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令}} (the separation of Shinto and Buddhism), the deities were relocated from the original {{ill|Miyama Shrine|ja|御山神社}} to the current Sanki-dō.
=== Origin === It is believed that the revered monk {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}} (also known as Kōbō Daishi) enshrined Sanki Daigongen in 806 CE (Daido 1) when he founded Mount Misen as a sacred site.
=== Mantra === The mantra associated with Sanki Daigongen is: * '''On Aratan Nō Un Sowaka''' (オン・アラタンノウ・ウン・ソワカ).
Additionally, there are {{ill|wasan|ja|和讃}} (Buddhist hymns) praising the virtues of Sanki Daigongen.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Buddhas|ja|仏の一覧}} * {{ill|Gongen|ja|権現}} * {{ill|Daitengu|ja|大天狗}} * {{ill|Mountain worship|ja|山岳信仰}} * {{ill|Mitaki-dera|ja|三瀧寺}} * {{ill|Guhin|ja|狗賓}}

== Sanki Daigongen == '''Sanki Daigongen''' (三鬼大権現) is a {{ill|demon deity|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:鬼神]] revered in {{ill|Tengu|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:天狗]] worship and {{ill|mountain Buddhism|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:山岳仏教]], particularly within the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:真言宗]] tradition. It is also known as '''Itsukushima Sanki-bō''' (厳島三鬼坊).
== Overview == Sanki Daigongen is primarily enshrined at the {{ill|Sanki-dō|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:三鬼堂]] on {{ill|Mount Misen|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:弥山 (広島県)]] and within the {{ill|Maniden|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:魔尼殿]] at {{ill|Daishō-in|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:大聖院 (宮島)]] on {{ill|Miyajima|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:宮島]], located in {{ill|Hatsukaichi|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:廿日市市]], {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:広島県]]. Sanki Daigongen is believed to command both great and small Tengu as retainers, using vast supernatural powers to save sentient beings. Locally, it is affectionately referred to as '''Sanki-san'''. It is said that {{ill|Itō Hirobumi|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:伊藤博文]], Japan’s first Prime Minister, was a devout follower.
The three deities of Sanki Daigongen are: * **Tsuichō Kishin (追帳鬼神)**: A deity governing the virtue of "fortune and virtue" (福徳), with {{ill|Dainichi Nyorai|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:大日如来]] as the {{ill|honji-butsu|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:本地仏]] (original Buddha). * **Jibi Kishin (時眉鬼神)**: A deity overseeing the virtue of "wisdom" (知恵), with {{ill|Kokūzō Bosatsu|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:虚空蔵菩薩]] as the honji-butsu. Kokūzō Bosatsu is also the principal deity enshrined at {{ill|Misen Main Hall|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:弥山本堂]]. * **Mara Kishin (魔羅鬼神)**: A deity associated with the virtue of "subjugation" (降伏), with {{ill|Fudō Myōō|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:不動明王]] as the honji-butsu.
Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:神仏分離令]] (the separation of Shinto and Buddhism), the deities were relocated from the original {{ill|Miyama Shrine|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:御山神社]] to the current Sanki-dō.
=== Origin === It is believed that the revered monk {{ill|Kūkai|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:空海]] (also known as Kōbō Daishi) enshrined Sanki Daigongen in 806 CE (Daido 1) when he founded Mount Misen as a sacred site.
=== Mantra === The mantra associated with Sanki Daigongen is: * '''On Aratan Nō Un Sowaka''' (オン・アラタンノウ・ウン・ソワカ).
Additionally, there are {{ill|wasan|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:和讃]] (Buddhist hymns) praising the virtues of Sanki Daigongen.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Buddhas|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:仏の一覧]] * {{ill|Gongen|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:権現]] * {{ill|Daitengu|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:大天狗]] * {{ill|Mountain worship|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:山岳信仰]] * {{ill|Mitaki-dera|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:三瀧寺]] * {{ill|Guhin|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:狗賓]]

== Sanki Daigongen == '''Sanki Daigongen''' (三鬼大権現) is a {{ill|demon deity|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:鬼神]] revered in {{ill|Tengu|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:天狗]] worship and {{ill|mountain Buddhism|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:山岳仏教]], particularly within the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:真言宗]] tradition. It is also known as '''Itsukushima Sanki-bō''' (厳島三鬼坊).
== Overview == Sanki Daigongen is primarily enshrined at the {{ill|Sanki-dō|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:三鬼堂]] on {{ill|Mount Misen|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:弥山 (広島県)]] and within the {{ill|Maniden|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:魔尼殿]] at {{ill|Daishō-in|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:大聖院 (宮島)]] on {{ill|Miyajima|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:宮島]], located in {{ill|Hatsukaichi|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:廿日市市]], {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:広島県]]. Sanki Daigongen is believed to command both great and small Tengu as retainers, using vast supernatural powers to save sentient beings. Locally, it is affectionately referred to as '''Sanki-san'''. It is said that {{ill|Itō Hirobumi|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:伊藤博文]], Japan’s first Prime Minister, was a devout follower.
The three deities of Sanki Daigongen are: * **Tsuichō Kishin (追帳鬼神)**: A deity governing the virtue of "fortune and virtue" (福徳), with {{ill|Dainichi Nyorai|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:大日如来]] as the {{ill|honji-butsu|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:本地仏]] (original Buddha). * **Jibi Kishin (時眉鬼神)**: A deity overseeing the virtue of "wisdom" (知恵), with {{ill|Kokūzō Bosatsu|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:虚空蔵菩薩]] as the honji-butsu. Kokūzō Bosatsu is also the principal deity enshrined at {{ill|Misen Main Hall|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:弥山本堂]]. * **Mara Kishin (魔羅鬼神)**: A deity associated with the virtue of "subjugation" (降伏), with {{ill|Fudō Myōō|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:不動明王]] as the honji-butsu.
Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:神仏分離令]] (the separation of Shinto and Buddhism), the deities were relocated from the original {{ill|Miyama Shrine|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:御山神社]] to the current Sanki-dō.
=== Origin === It is believed that the revered monk {{ill|Kūkai|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:空海]] (also known as Kōbō Daishi) enshrined Sanki Daigongen in 806 CE (Daido 1) when he founded Mount Misen as a sacred site.
=== Mantra === The mantra associated with Sanki Daigongen is: * '''On Aratan Nō Un Sowaka''' (オン・アラタンノウ・ウン・ソワカ).
Additionally, there are {{ill|wasan|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:和讃]] (Buddhist hymns) praising the virtues of Sanki Daigongen.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Buddhas|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:仏の一覧]] * {{ill|Gongen|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:権現]] * {{ill|Daitengu|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:大天狗]] * {{ill|Mountain worship|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:山岳信仰]] * {{ill|Mitaki-dera|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:三瀧寺]] * {{ill|Guhin|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:狗賓]]

Kokūzō Bosatsu is also the principal deity enshrined at {{ill|Misen Main Hall|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Demon deity-qq]] [[ja:弥山本堂]]. * **Mara Kishin (魔羅鬼神)**: A deity associated with the virtue of "subjugation" (降伏), with {{ill|Fudō Myōō|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Demon deity-qq]] [[ja:不動明王]] as the honji-butsu.

'''Kishin''' (鬼神, {{lang-en|''Demonic Gods'' or ''Spiritual Deities''}}) refers to supernatural entities in various cultural traditions. When pronounced as "kijin" or "kishin," it primarily denotes spirits or deities of the heavens and earth. When read as "onigami," it refers to unseen spirits or fierce and terrifying gods.{{Sfn|Kotobank|2021}} == China == {{Chinese cultural elements | pinyin = guǐshén | katakana = グイシェン | hiragana = きしん}} In {{ill|China|ja|中国|12=draft}}, the term "鬼神" (guǐshén) refers to two contrasting types of spirits: "鬼" (guǐ, ghosts) and "神" (shén, gods). * **Gui (鬼)** – Malevolent spirits that bring misfortune to the living, manifesting as {{ill|evil ghosts|ja|悪鬼|12=draft}}. They are often the subject of exorcisms and prayers.{{Sfn|Makitani|2021}} Gui are the posthumous forms of the "魄" (pò), the yin-energy spirit of a living person, which governs the physical body. When a person dies, their pò remains in the earthly realm and transforms into a ghost. If they died a violent death or were left without a proper burial and veneration, they might become vengeful ghosts (幽鬼) and cause calamities.{{Sfn|Watanabe|2021}} * **Shen (神)** – Benevolent spirits that bring blessings to the living and are worshiped in {{ill|rituals|ja|祭祀|12=draft}}.{{Sfn|Makitani|2021}} Shen originate from the "魂" (hún), the yang-energy spirit of a living person, which governs the mind and ascends to the heavens after death, becoming a deity or divine spirit.{{Sfn|Makitani|2021}}{{Sfn|Watanabe|2021}} Additionally, the term "鬼神" can refer to the mysterious forces beyond human comprehension that create, change, and destroy all things in the universe.{{Sfn|Makitani|2021}} == Korea == {{Korean cultural elements | hangeul = 귀신 | katakana = クィシン}} In the {{ill|folk beliefs|ja|民間信仰|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|朝鮮半島|12=draft}}, "gwi-shin" (귀신) refers to spirits that, unlike ancestral or guardian spirits, do not have a designated host and exist as independent entities.{{Sfn|GWE|2021}}{{Sfn|Soul Worship|2021}} This distinguishes them from spirits that reside within humans or natural elements, such as deities (신명, sinmyeong). However, over time, the distinction between these categories has blurred.{{Sfn|GWE|2021}}{{Sfn|Soul Worship|2021}} Gwi-shin are depicted as weak and ghostly beings, clad in fragile paper garments, with emaciated legs like glue-covered bones. They are powerless before divine beings and can shrink away when confronted by strong-willed humans.{{Sfn|GWE|2021}} Though typically invisible, they may be appeased with offerings of food. However, some gwi-shin are malevolent and bring misfortune.{{Sfn|GWE|2021}} During the daytime, gwi-shin wander through the air aimlessly, but at night, they seek damp places to rest.{{Sfn|GWE|2021}} They often reside in ancient trees, shadowy groves, underground spaces, or near wells and ponds. To keep them away, people scatter {{ill|salt|ja|塩|12=draft}} or {{ill|beans|ja|豆|12=draft}}.{{Sfn|GWE|2021}} Some gwi-shin have supernatural foresight and can predict calamities, warn of impending doom, or reveal the location of lost objects.{{Sfn|GWE|2021}} However, they are generally depicted as being driven away by more powerful entities. In {{ill|Korean folklore|ja|朝鮮の伝説|12=draft}}, figures like {{ill|Cheoyong|ko|처용|12=draft}}, who expelled evil spirits, appear on protective talismans (符籍) to ward off gwi-shin.{{Sfn|GWE|2021}} Additionally, stronger gwi-shin may prey upon weaker ones.{{Sfn|GWE|2021}} It is also believed that even divine spirits can turn into gwi-shin if they lose their place of worship or are forgotten by the living.{{Sfn|GWE|2021}} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Global World Encyclopedia|publisher=Doosan Corporation}} * {{Cite web|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%AC%BC%E7%A5%9E-50543#E7.B2.BE.E9.81.B8.E7.89.88.20.E6.97.A5.E6.9C.AC.E5.9B.BD.E8.AA.9E.E5.A4.A7.E8.BE.9E.E5.85.B8 |title=鬼神 |publisher=Shogakukan |work=Nihon Kokugo Daijiten |date=2021-07-16 |language=ja}} * {{Cite web|url=https://ko.wikisource.org/wiki/%EA%B8%80%EB%A1%9C%EB%B2%8C_%EC%84%B8%EA%B3%84_%EB%8C%80%EB%B0%B1%EA%B3%BC%EC%82%AC%EC%A0%84/%EC%A2%85%EA%B5%90%C2%B7%EC%B2%A0%ED%95%99/%ED%95%9C%EA%B5%AD%EC%9D%98_%EC%A2%85%EA%B5%90/%EB%AF%BC_%EA%B0%84_%EC%8B%A0_%EC%95%99/%EC%88%AD_%EB%B0%B0#%EA%B7%80%EC%8B%A0 |title=귀신 (鬼神) |publisher=Global World Encyclopedia |date=2021-07-16 |language=ko}} == See Also == * {{ill|Arai Hakuseki|ja|新井白石|12=draft}}'s *{{ill|On Kishin|ja|鬼神論|12=draft}}* * {{ill|Yamakata Bantō|ja|山片蟠桃|12=draft}}'s *{{ill|Yume no Shiro|ja|夢の代|12=draft}}* * {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤|12=draft}}'s *{{ill|Kishin Shinron|ja|鬼神新論|12=draft}}*

the living.{{Sfn|GWE|2021}} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Global World Encyclopedia|publisher=Doosan Corporation}} * {{Cite web|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%AC%BC%E7%A5%9E-50543#E7.B2.BE.E9.81.B8.E7.89.88.20.E6.97.A5.E6.9C.AC.E5.9B.BD.E8.AA.9E.E5.A4.A7.E8.BE.9E.E5.85.B8 |title=鬼神 |publisher=Shogakukan |work=Nihon '''Kishin''' (鬼神, {{lang-en|''Demonic Gods'' or ''Spiritual Deities''}}) refers to supernatural entities in various cultural traditions. When pronounced as "kijin" or "kishin," it primarily denotes spirits or deities of the heavens and earth. When read as "onigami," it refers to unseen spirits or fierce and terrifying gods.{{Sfn|Kotobank|2021}} == China == {{Chinese cultural elements | pinyin = guǐshén | katakana = グイシェン | hiragana = きしん}} In {{ill|China|ja|中国|12=draft}}, the term "鬼神" (guǐshén) refers to two contrasting types of spirits: "鬼" (guǐ, ghosts) and "神" (shén, gods). * **Gui (鬼)** – Malevolent spirits that bring misfortune to the living, manifesting as {{ill|evil ghosts|ja|悪鬼|12=draft}}. They are often the subject of exorcisms and prayers.{{Sfn|Makitani|2021}} Gui are the posthumous forms of the "魄" (pò), the yin-energy spirit of a living person, which governs the physical body. When a person dies, their pò remains in the earthly realm and transforms into a ghost. If they died a violent death or were left without a proper burial and veneration, they might become vengeful ghosts (幽鬼) and cause calamities.{{Sfn|Watanabe|2021}} * **Shen (神)** – Benevolent spirits that bring blessings to the living and are worshiped in {{ill|rituals|ja|祭祀|12=draft}}.{{Sfn|Makitani|2021}} Shen originate from the "魂" (hún), the yang-energy spirit of a living person, which governs the mind and ascends to the heavens after death, becoming a deity or divine spirit.{{Sfn|Makitani|2021}}{{Sfn|Watanabe|2021}} Additionally, the term "鬼神" can refer to the mysterious forces beyond human comprehension that create, change, and destroy all things in the universe.{{Sfn|Makitani|2021}} == Korea == {{Korean cultural elements | hangeul = 귀신 | katakana = クィシン}} In the {{ill|folk beliefs|ja|民間信仰|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|朝鮮半島|12=draft}}, "gwi-shin" (귀신) refers to spirits that, unlike ancestral or guardian spirits, do not have a designated host and exist as independent entities.{{Sfn|GWE|2021}}{{Sfn|Soul Worship|2021}} This distinguishes them from spirits that reside within humans or natural elements, such as deities (신명, sinmyeong). However, over time, the distinction between these categories has blurred.{{Sfn|GWE|2021}}{{Sfn|Soul Worship|2021}} Gwi-shin are depicted as weak and ghostly beings, clad in fragile paper garments, with emaciated legs like glue-covered bones. They are powerless before divine beings and can shrink away when confronted by strong-willed humans.{{Sfn|GWE|2021}} Though typically invisible, they may be appeased with offerings of food. However, some gwi-shin are malevolent and bring misfortune.{{Sfn|GWE|2021}} During the daytime, gwi-shin wander through the air aimlessly, but at night, they seek damp places to rest.{{Sfn|GWE|2021}} They often reside in ancient trees, shadowy groves, underground spaces, or near wells and ponds. To keep them away, people scatter {{ill|salt|ja|塩|12=draft}} or {{ill|beans|ja|豆|12=draft}}.{{Sfn|GWE|2021}} Some gwi-shin have supernatural foresight and can predict calamities, warn of impending doom, or reveal the location of lost objects.{{Sfn|GWE|2021}} However, they are generally depicted as being driven away by more powerful entities. In {{ill|Korean folklore|ja|朝鮮の伝説|12=draft}}, figures like {{ill|Cheoyong|ko|처용|12=draft}}, who expelled evil spirits, appear on protective talismans (符籍) to ward off gwi-shin.{{Sfn|GWE|2021}} Additionally, stronger gwi-shin may prey upon weaker ones.{{Sfn|GWE|2021}} It is also believed that even divine spirits can turn into gwi-shin if they lose their place of worship or are forgotten by the living.{{Sfn|GWE|2021}} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Global World Encyclopedia|publisher=Doosan Corporation}} * {{Cite web|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%AC%BC%E7%A5%9E-50543#E7.B2.BE.E9.81.B8.E7.89.88.20.E6.97.A5.E6.9C.AC.E5.9B.BD.E8.AA.9E.E5.A4.A7.E8.BE.9E.E5.85.B8 |title=鬼神 |publisher=Shogakukan |work=Nihon Kokugo Daijiten |date=2021-07-16 |language=ja}} * {{Cite web|url=https://ko.wikisource.org/wiki/%EA%B8%80%EB%A1%9C%EB%B2%8C_%EC%84%B8%EA%B3%84_%EB%8C%80%EB%B0%B1%EA%B3%BC%EC%82%AC%EC%A0%84/%EC%A2%85%EA%B5%90%C2%B7%EC%B2%A0%ED%95%99/%ED%95%9C%EA%B5%AD%EC%9D%98_%EC%A2%85%EA%B5%90/%EB%AF%BC_%EA%B0%84_%EC%8B%A0_%EC%95%99/%EC%88%AD_%EB%B0%B0#%EA%B7%80%EC%8B%A0 |title=귀신 (鬼神) |publisher=Global World Encyclopedia |date=2021-07-16 |language=ko}} == See Also == * {{ill|Arai Hakuseki|ja|新井白石|12=draft}}'s *{{ill|On Kishin|ja|鬼神論|12=draft}}* * {{ill|Yamakata '''Kishin''' (鬼神, {{lang-en|''Demonic Gods'' or ''Spiritual Deities''}}) refers to supernatural entities in various cultural traditions. When pronounced as "kijin" or "kishin," it primarily denotes spirits or deities of the heavens and earth. When read as "onigami," it refers to unseen spirits or fierce and terrifying gods.{{Sfn|Kotobank|2021}} == China == {{Chinese cultural elements | pinyin = guǐshén | katakana = グイシェン | hiragana = きしん}} In {{ill|China|ja|中国|12=draft}}, the term "鬼神" (guǐshén) refers to two contrasting types of spirits: "鬼" (guǐ, ghosts) and "神" (shén, gods). * **Gui (鬼)** – Malevolent spirits that bring misfortune to the living, manifesting as {{ill|evil ghosts|ja|悪鬼|12=draft}}. They are often the subject of exorcisms and prayers.{{Sfn|Makitani|2021}} Gui are the posthumous forms of the "魄" (pò), the yin-energy spirit of a living person, which governs the physical body. When a person dies, their pò remains in the earthly realm and transforms into a ghost. If they died a violent death or were left without a proper burial and veneration, they might become vengeful ghosts (幽鬼) and cause calamities.{{Sfn|Watanabe|2021}} * **Shen (神)** – Benevolent spirits that bring blessings to the living and are worshiped in {{ill|rituals|ja|祭祀|12=draft}}.{{Sfn|Makitani|2021}} Shen originate from the "魂" (hún), the yang-energy spirit of a living person, which governs the mind and ascends to the heavens after death, becoming a deity or divine spirit.{{Sfn|Makitani|2021}}{{Sfn|Watanabe|2021}} Additionally, the term "鬼神" can refer to the mysterious forces beyond human comprehension that create, change, and destroy all things in the universe.{{Sfn|Makitani|2021}} == Korea == {{Korean cultural elements | hangeul = 귀신 | katakana = クィシン}} In the {{ill|folk beliefs|ja|民間信仰|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|朝鮮半島|12=draft}}, "gwi-shin" (귀신) refers to spirits that, unlike ancestral or guardian spirits, do not have a designated host and exist as independent entities.{{Sfn|GWE|2021}}{{Sfn|Soul Worship|2021}} This distinguishes them from spirits that reside within humans or natural elements, such as deities (신명, sinmyeong). However, over time, the distinction between these categories has blurred.{{Sfn|GWE|2021}}{{Sfn|Soul Worship|2021}} Gwi-shin are depicted as weak and ghostly beings, clad in fragile paper garments, with emaciated legs like glue-covered bones. They are powerless before divine beings and can shrink away when confronted by strong-willed humans.{{Sfn|GWE|2021}} Though typically invisible, they may be appeased with offerings of food. However, some gwi-shin are malevolent and bring misfortune.{{Sfn|GWE|2021}} During the daytime, gwi-shin wander through the air aimlessly, but at night, they seek damp places to rest.{{Sfn|GWE|2021}} They often reside in ancient trees, shadowy groves, underground spaces, or near wells and ponds. To keep them away, people scatter {{ill|salt|ja|塩|12=draft}} or {{ill|beans|ja|豆|12=draft}}.{{Sfn|GWE|2021}} Some gwi-shin have supernatural foresight and can predict calamities, warn of impending doom, or reveal the location of lost objects.{{Sfn|GWE|2021}} However, they are generally depicted as being driven away by more powerful entities. In {{ill|Korean folklore|ja|朝鮮の伝説|12=draft}}, figures like {{ill|Cheoyong|ko|처용|12=draft}}, who expelled evil spirits, appear on protective talismans (符籍) to ward off gwi-shin.{{Sfn|GWE|2021}} Additionally, stronger gwi-shin may prey upon weaker ones.{{Sfn|GWE|2021}} It is also believed that even divine spirits can turn into gwi-shin if they lose their place of worship or are forgotten by the living.{{Sfn|GWE|2021}} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Global World Encyclopedia|publisher=Doosan Corporation}} * {{Cite web|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%AC%BC%E7%A5%9E-50543#E7.B2.BE.E9.81.B8.E7.89.88.20.E6.97.A5.E6.9C.AC.E5.9B.BD.E8.AA.9E.E5.A4.A7.E8.BE.9E.E5.85.B8 |title=鬼神 |publisher=Shogakukan |work=Nihon Kokugo Daijiten |date=2021-07-16 |language=ja}} * {{Cite web|url=https://ko.wikisource.org/wiki/%EA%B8%80%EB%A1%9C%EB%B2%8C_%EC%84%B8%EA%B3%84_%EB%8C%80%EB%B0%B1%EA%B3%BC%EC%82%AC%EC%A0%84/%EC%A2%85%EA%B5%90%C2%B7%EC%B2%A0%ED%95%99/%ED%95%9C%EA%B5%AD%EC%9D%98_%EC%A2%85%EA%B5%90/%EB%AF%BC_%EA%B0%84_%EC%8B%A0_%EC%95%99/%EC%88%AD_%EB%B0%B0#%EA%B7%80%EC%8B%A0 |title=귀신 (鬼神) |publisher=Global World Encyclopedia |date=2021-07-16 |language=ko}} == See Also == * {{ill|Arai Hakuseki|ja|新井白石|12=draft}}'s *{{ill|On Kishin|ja|鬼神論|12=draft}}* * {{ill|Yamakata [[Category:Oni]] [[Category:Sanki Daigongen forks]] [[Category:Ghosts in East Asia]] [[Category:Shinto deities]] [[Category:Chinese deities]] [[Category:Korean folklore]] [[Category:New]] }} revision.text:
'''鬼神'''（きじん、きしん、おにがみ）は、「きじん」または「きしん」と音読みした場合の第一義としては天地万物の[[霊魂]]あるいは[[神々]]を意味し、「おにがみ」と訓読みした場合は目に見えない精霊または荒々しく恐ろしい神を意味する<ref>「[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%AC%BC%E7%A5%9E-50543#E7.B2.BE.E9.81.B8.E7.89.88.20.E6.97.A5.E6.9C.AC.E5.9B.BD.E8.AA.9E.E5.A4.A7.E8.BE.9E.E5.85.B8 鬼神]」『精選版 [[日本国語大辞典]]』[[小学館]]、[[コトバンク]]。2021年7月16日閲覧。</ref>。
== 中国 == {{中華圏の事物 | ピン音 = guǐshén | カタカナ = グイシェン | ひらがな = きしん}} [[中国]]における鬼神は対照的な2つの[[霊]]である鬼と神を指す。
鬼（き）は生者に禍（わざわい）をもたらす霊であり、[[悪鬼]]として顕現し、祈祓（きふつ、いのりはらい）の対象である<ref name="麦谷邦夫" />。鬼は生者が持つ魄（はく）の死後の姿であり、魄は生者の内にあって肉体をつかさどる陰気の霊であり、その魄が死後に地上にとどまって鬼となる<ref name="麦谷邦夫" />。その死が[[横死]]であったり、身寄りがなく死後に祀（まつ）る者のない場合に悪鬼・幽鬼として生者に祟（たた）る<ref name="渡邊昭五">[[渡邊昭五]]「[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%AC%BC%28%E3%81%8D%29-1522360 鬼（き）]」『[[日本大百科全書]](ニッポニカ)』[[小学館]]、コトバンク。2021年7月23日閲覧。</ref>。
神（しん）は生者に福をもたらす霊であり、善神として顕現し、[[祭祀]]の対象である<ref name="麦谷邦夫" />。神は生者が持つ[[魂]]の死後の姿であり、魂は生者の内にあって精神をつかさどる陽気の霊であり、その魂が天上に昇って神・[[神霊]]となる<ref name="麦谷邦夫">「[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%AC%BC%E7%A5%9E-50543#E4.B8.96.E7.95.8C.E5.A4.A7.E7.99.BE.E7.A7.91.E4.BA.8B.E5.85.B8.20.E7.AC.AC.EF.BC.92.E7.89.88 鬼神]」『[[世界大百科事典]]』第2版、[[平凡社]]、[[コトバンク]]。2021年7月23日閲覧。</ref><ref name="渡邊昭五" />。
また、鬼神は天地のあらゆる物の創造・変化・消滅にかかる人知の及ばぬ霊妙な働きを指す場合がある<ref name="麦谷邦夫" />。
== 朝鮮 == {{朝鮮の事物 | hangeul = 귀신 | katakana = クィシン}} [[朝鮮半島]]の[[民間信仰]]における鬼神は、生者あるいは死者の内にある[[霊]]とは異なり、宿る対象を持たず、何にも従属しない孤立した存在である<ref name="GWE鬼神" /><ref name="靈魂崇拜" />。この点で鬼神は人に宿る霊や人以外に宿る[[精]]（精霊）あるいは{{仮リンク|神明 (朝鮮)|label=神明|ko|신명 (신령)}}と区別されるが、宿る対象についての観念がはっきりしなくなると霊や精あるいは神明と区別されなくなる<ref name="GWE鬼神" /><ref name="靈魂崇拜">「[https://ko.wikisource.org/wiki/%EA%B8%80%EB%A1%9C%EB%B2%8C_%EC%84%B8%EA%B3%84_%EB%8C%80%EB%B0%B1%EA%B3%BC%EC%82%AC%EC%A0%84/%EC%A2%85%EA%B5%90%C2%B7%EC%B2%A0%ED%95%99/%ED%95%9C%EA%B5%AD%EC%9D%98_%EC%A2%85%EA%B5%90/%EB%AF%BC_%EA%B0%84_%EC%8B%A0_%EC%95%99/%EC%88%AD_%EB%B0%B0#%EC%98%81%ED%98%BC%EC%88%AD%EB%B0%B0 靈魂崇拜]」『グローバル世界大百科事典』ウィキソース。2021年7月16日閲覧。</ref>。
鬼神は腰より下が[[障子紙]]の服で、足はやせ細りまるで[[にかわ]]のようで骨だけ残った姿をしていて、まもなく病を得て死ぬ身の上であり、神明の前で立ちすくんでしまう力弱い存在で、力の強い人に睨まれるとだんだん小さくなって消えうせる存在である<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。人が与える食べ物におとなしくして話を聞く見えない存在だが、鬼神のなかには意地悪をするものがある<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。
鬼神は昼間はあちこち空中でふらふらするが、夜はじめじめした所を探して体を休めることもする<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。だいたい古木が鬼神の住みかになる<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。また人家を訪れるときがあるが、そのとき人は鬼神が嫌う方法を使って入って来ないように防いで、[[塩]]を撒いたり[[豆]]を撒いたりする<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。鬼神の住みかは藪の中や土の中、池、井戸端などじめじめした所などで、どこでも出入りすることが出来る<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。鬼神はいったん人の家に入ると食べ物を提供されてそこを去る。白昼に石を投げる乱暴を働いたり、時に猟犬の吠える声を出したり口笛を吹いたりして強風を起こしたりする<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。夜には道を通る人を怖がらせて火遊びもする<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。この火を鬼火（[[トッケビ]]の火）とも言い人々が怖がる<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。
鬼神は時に賢く、一国や一家族の滅亡を予言したり警告したり、あるいは遺失物の在りかを知らせたりもする<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。しかしだいたい鬼神は前述のごとく、より勢力があるものに追われる身なのである<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。[[疫病神]]を追い払ったという説話で知られる{{仮リンク|処容|ko|처용}}のような強い人物を描いたお守り（符籍）の前では立ちすくんでしまい、追い払われる存在である<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。また鬼神のうちで強い鬼神は弱い鬼神を殺害したりする<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。なお神明も泊まる居場所がなかったり、生存者から供え物をもらえないと鬼神になる場合がある<ref name="GWE鬼神">「[https://ko.wikisource.org/wiki/%EA%B8%80%EB%A1%9C%EB%B2%8C_%EC%84%B8%EA%B3%84_%EB%8C%80%EB%B0%B1%EA%B3%BC%EC%82%AC%EC%A0%84/%EC%A2%85%EA%B5%90%C2%B7%EC%B2%A0%ED%95%99/%ED%95%9C%EA%B5%AD%EC%9D%98_%EC%A2%85%EA%B5%90/%EB%AF%BC_%EA%B0%84_%EC%8B%A0_%EC%95%99/%EC%88%AD_%EB%B0%B0#%EA%B7%80%EC%8B%A0 鬼神]」『グローバル世界大百科事典』ウィキソース。2021年7月16日閲覧。</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *{{グローバル百科}}
== 関連項目 == *[[新井白石]]『[[鬼神論]]』 *[[山片蟠桃#思想]]『[[夢の代]]』 *[[平田篤胤]]『[[鬼神新論]]』

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[鬼神]] to [[Demon deity]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
'''鬼神'''（きじん、きしん、おにがみ）は、「きじん」または「きしん」と音読みした場合の第一義としては天地万物の[[霊魂]]あるいは[[神々]]を意味し、「おにがみ」と訓読みした場合は目に見えない精霊または荒々しく恐ろしい神を意味する<ref>「[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%AC%BC%E7%A5%9E-50543#E7.B2.BE.E9.81.B8.E7.89.88.20.E6.97.A5.E6.9C.AC.E5.9B.BD.E8.AA.9E.E5.A4.A7.E8.BE.9E.E5.85.B8 鬼神]」『精選版 [[日本国語大辞典]]』[[小学館]]、[[コトバンク]]。2021年7月16日閲覧。</ref>。
== 中国 == {{中華圏の事物 | ピン音 = guǐshén | カタカナ = グイシェン | ひらがな = きしん}} [[中国]]における鬼神は対照的な2つの[[霊]]である鬼と神を指す。
鬼（き）は生者に禍（わざわい）をもたらす霊であり、[[悪鬼]]として顕現し、祈祓（きふつ、いのりはらい）の対象である<ref name="麦谷邦夫" />。鬼は生者が持つ魄（はく）の死後の姿であり、魄は生者の内にあって肉体をつかさどる陰気の霊であり、その魄が死後に地上にとどまって鬼となる<ref name="麦谷邦夫" />。その死が[[横死]]であったり、身寄りがなく死後に祀（まつ）る者のない場合に悪鬼・幽鬼として生者に祟（たた）る<ref name="渡邊昭五">[[渡邊昭五]]「[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%AC%BC%28%E3%81%8D%29-1522360 鬼（き）]」『[[日本大百科全書]](ニッポニカ)』[[小学館]]、コトバンク。2021年7月23日閲覧。</ref>。
神（しん）は生者に福をもたらす霊であり、善神として顕現し、[[祭祀]]の対象である<ref name="麦谷邦夫" />。神は生者が持つ[[魂]]の死後の姿であり、魂は生者の内にあって精神をつかさどる陽気の霊であり、その魂が天上に昇って神・[[神霊]]となる<ref name="麦谷邦夫">「[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%AC%BC%E7%A5%9E-50543#E4.B8.96.E7.95.8C.E5.A4.A7.E7.99.BE.E7.A7.91.E4.BA.8B.E5.85.B8.20.E7.AC.AC.EF.BC.92.E7.89.88 鬼神]」『[[世界大百科事典]]』第2版、[[平凡社]]、[[コトバンク]]。2021年7月23日閲覧。</ref><ref name="渡邊昭五" />。
また、鬼神は天地のあらゆる物の創造・変化・消滅にかかる人知の及ばぬ霊妙な働きを指す場合がある<ref name="麦谷邦夫" />。
== 朝鮮 == {{朝鮮の事物 | hangeul = 귀신 | katakana = クィシン}} [[朝鮮半島]]の[[民間信仰]]における鬼神は、生者あるいは死者の内にある[[霊]]とは異なり、宿る対象を持たず、何にも従属しない孤立した存在である<ref name="GWE鬼神" /><ref name="靈魂崇拜" />。この点で鬼神は人に宿る霊や人以外に宿る[[精]]（精霊）あるいは{{仮リンク|神明 (朝鮮)|label=神明|ko|신명 (신령)}}と区別されるが、宿る対象についての観念がはっきりしなくなると霊や精あるいは神明と区別されなくなる<ref name="GWE鬼神" /><ref name="靈魂崇拜">「[https://ko.wikisource.org/wiki/%EA%B8%80%EB%A1%9C%EB%B2%8C_%EC%84%B8%EA%B3%84_%EB%8C%80%EB%B0%B1%EA%B3%BC%EC%82%AC%EC%A0%84/%EC%A2%85%EA%B5%90%C2%B7%EC%B2%A0%ED%95%99/%ED%95%9C%EA%B5%AD%EC%9D%98_%EC%A2%85%EA%B5%90/%EB%AF%BC_%EA%B0%84_%EC%8B%A0_%EC%95%99/%EC%88%AD_%EB%B0%B0#%EC%98%81%ED%98%BC%EC%88%AD%EB%B0%B0 靈魂崇拜]」『グローバル世界大百科事典』ウィキソース。2021年7月16日閲覧。</ref>。
鬼神は腰より下が[[障子紙]]の服で、足はやせ細りまるで[[にかわ]]のようで骨だけ残った姿をしていて、まもなく病を得て死ぬ身の上であり、神明の前で立ちすくんでしまう力弱い存在で、力の強い人に睨まれるとだんだん小さくなって消えうせる存在である<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。人が与える食べ物におとなしくして話を聞く見えない存在だが、鬼神のなかには意地悪をするものがある<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。
鬼神は昼間はあちこち空中でふらふらするが、夜はじめじめした所を探して体を休めることもする<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。だいたい古木が鬼神の住みかになる<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。また人家を訪れるときがあるが、そのとき人は鬼神が嫌う方法を使って入って来ないように防いで、[[塩]]を撒いたり[[豆]]を撒いたりする<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。鬼神の住みかは藪の中や土の中、池、井戸端などじめじめした所などで、どこでも出入りすることが出来る<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。鬼神はいったん人の家に入ると食べ物を提供されてそこを去る。白昼に石を投げる乱暴を働いたり、時に猟犬の吠える声を出したり口笛を吹いたりして強風を起こしたりする<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。夜には道を通る人を怖がらせて火遊びもする<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。この火を鬼火（[[トッケビ]]の火）とも言い人々が怖がる<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。
鬼神は時に賢く、一国や一家族の滅亡を予言したり警告したり、あるいは遺失物の在りかを知らせたりもする<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。しかしだいたい鬼神は前述のごとく、より勢力があるものに追われる身なのである<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。[[疫病神]]を追い払ったという説話で知られる{{仮リンク|処容|ko|처용}}のような強い人物を描いたお守り（符籍）の前では立ちすくんでしまい、追い払われる存在である<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。また鬼神のうちで強い鬼神は弱い鬼神を殺害したりする<ref name="GWE鬼神" />。なお神明も泊まる居場所がなかったり、生存者から供え物をもらえないと鬼神になる場合がある<ref name="GWE鬼神">「[https://ko.wikisource.org/wiki/%EA%B8%80%EB%A1%9C%EB%B2%8C_%EC%84%B8%EA%B3%84_%EB%8C%80%EB%B0%B1%EA%B3%BC%EC%82%AC%EC%A0%84/%EC%A2%85%EA%B5%90%C2%B7%EC%B2%A0%ED%95%99/%ED%95%9C%EA%B5%AD%EC%9D%98_%EC%A2%85%EA%B5%90/%EB%AF%BC_%EA%B0%84_%EC%8B%A0_%EC%95%99/%EC%88%AD_%EB%B0%B0#%EA%B7%80%EC%8B%A0 鬼神]」『グローバル世界大百科事典』ウィキソース。2021年7月16日閲覧。</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *{{グローバル百科}}
== 関連項目 == *[[新井白石]]『[[鬼神論]]』 *[[山片蟠桃#思想]]『[[夢の代]]』 *[[平田篤胤]]『[[鬼神新論]]』
{{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きしん}} [[Category:鬼]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Gwisin]][[es:Gwishin]][[fr:Gwishin]][[hu:Küsin]][[id:Gwishin]][[ja:鬼神]][[ko:귀신]][[zh:鬼神]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q5623686]] Wikidata name: Gwisin [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=There is an enwiki article but it seems horribly incomplete and possibly completely wrong scope[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 17:58, 13 February 2025 (UTC)|qq=}}
*
'''Kishin''' (鬼神, {{lang-en|''Demonic Gods'' or ''Spiritual Deities''|qq=}}) refers to supernatural entities in various cultural traditions. When pronounced as "kijin" or "kishin," it primarily denotes spirits or deities of the heavens and earth. When read as "onigami," it refers to unseen spirits or fierce and terrifying gods.{{Sfn|Kotobank|2021|qq=}} == China == {{Chinese cultural elements | pinyin = guǐshén | katakana = グイシェン | hiragana = きしん|qq=}} In {{ill|China|ja|中国|1=China|2=ja|3=中国|lt=China|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/China|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29520}}, the term "鬼神" (guǐshén) refers to two contrasting types of spirits: "鬼" (guǐ, ghosts) and "神" (shén, gods). * '''Gui (鬼)''' – Malevolent spirits that bring misfortune to the living, manifesting as {{ill|evil ghosts|ja|悪鬼|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/evil ghosts|qq=|1=evil ghosts|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492603}}. They are often the subject of exorcisms and prayers.{{Sfn|Makitani|2021|qq=}} Gui are the posthumous forms of the "魄" (pò), the yin-energy spirit of a living person, which governs the physical body. When a person dies, their pò remains in the earthly realm and transforms into a ghost. If they died a violent death or were left without a proper burial and veneration, they might become vengeful ghosts (幽鬼) and cause calamities.{{Sfn|Watanabe|2021|qq=}} * '''Shen (神)''' – Benevolent spirits that bring blessings to the living and are worshiped in {{ill|rituals|ja|祭祀|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rituals|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭|1=festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132241}}.{{Sfn|Makitani|2021|qq=}} Shen originate from the "魂" (hún), the yang-energy spirit of a living person, which governs the mind and ascends to the heavens after death, becoming a deity or divine spirit.{{Sfn|Makitani|2021|qq=}}{{Sfn|Watanabe|2021|qq=}} Additionally, the term "鬼神" can refer to the mysterious forces beyond human comprehension that create, change, and destroy all things in the universe.{{Sfn|Makitani|2021|qq=}} == Korea == {{Korean cultural elements | hangeul = 귀신 | katakana = クィシン|qq=}} In the [[:en:Folk religion|folk beliefs]] of the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|朝鮮半島|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Korean Peninsula|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Korean Peninsula|WD=Q483134}}, "gwi-shin" (귀신) refers to spirits that, unlike ancestral or guardian spirits, do not have a designated host and exist as independent entities.{{Sfn|GWE|2021|qq=}}{{Sfn|Soul Worship|2021|qq=}} This distinguishes them from spirits that reside within humans or natural elements, such as deities (신명, sinmyeong). However, over time, the distinction between these categories has blurred.{{Sfn|GWE|2021|qq=}}{{Sfn|Soul Worship|2021|qq=}} Gwi-shin are depicted as weak and ghostly beings, clad in fragile paper garments, with emaciated legs like glue-covered bones. They are powerless before divine beings and can shrink away when confronted by strong-willed humans.{{Sfn|GWE|2021|qq=}} Though typically invisible, they may be appeased with offerings of food. However, some gwi-shin are malevolent and bring misfortune.{{Sfn|GWE|2021|qq=}} During the daytime, gwi-shin wander through the air aimlessly, but at night, they seek damp places to rest.{{Sfn|GWE|2021|qq=}} They often reside in ancient trees, shadowy groves, underground spaces, or near wells and ponds. To keep them away, people scatter [[:en:Salt|salt]] or [[:en:Bean|beans]].{{Sfn|GWE|2021|qq=}} Some gwi-shin have supernatural foresight and can predict calamities, warn of impending doom, or reveal the location of lost objects.{{Sfn|GWE|2021|qq=}} However, they are generally depicted as being driven away by more powerful entities. In {{ill|Korean folklore|ja|朝鮮の伝説|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Korean folklore|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, figures like {{ill|Cheoyong|ko|처용|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cheoyong|qq=}}, who expelled evil spirits, appear on protective talismans (符籍) to ward off gwi-shin.{{Sfn|GWE|2021|qq=}} Additionally, stronger gwi-shin may prey upon weaker ones.{{Sfn|GWE|2021|qq=}} It is also believed that even divine spirits can turn into gwi-shin if they lose their place of worship or are forgotten by the living.{{Sfn|GWE|2021|qq=}} == References == {{reflist|qq=}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Global World Encyclopedia|publisher=Doosan Corporation|qq=}} * {{Cite web|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%AC%BC%E7%A5%9E-50543#E7.B2.BE.E9.81.B8.E7.89.88.20.E6.97.A5.E6.9C.AC.E5.9B.BD.E8.AA.9E.E5.A4.A7.E8.BE.9E.E5.85.B8 |title=鬼神 |publisher=Shogakukan |work=Nihon Kokugo Daijiten |date=2021-07-16 |language=ja|qq=}} * {{Cite web|url=https://ko.wikisource.org/wiki/%EA%B8%80%EB%A1%9C%EB%B2%8C_%EC%84%B8%EA%B3%84_%EB%8C%80%EB%B0%B1%EA%B3%BC%EC%82%AC%EC%A0%84/%EC%A2%85%EA%B5%90%C2%B7%EC%B2%A0%ED%95%99/%ED%95%9C%EA%B5%AD%EC%9D%98_%EC%A2%85%EA%B5%90/%EB%AF%BC_%EA%B0%84_%EC%8B%A0_%EC%95%99/%EC%88%AD_%EB%B0%B0#%EA%B7%80%EC%8B%A0 |title=귀신 (鬼神) |publisher=Global World Encyclopedia |date=2021-07-16 |language=ko|qq=}} == See Also == * [[:en:Arai Hakuseki|Arai Hakuseki]]'s {{ill|On Kishin|ja|鬼神論|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/On Kishin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Yamagata Bantō|Yamakata Bantō]]'s {{ill|Yume no Shiro|ja|夢の代|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yume no Shiro|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]]'s {{ill|Kishin Shinron|ja|鬼神新論|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishin Shinron|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

To keep them away, people scatter [[:en:Salt|salt]] or [[:en:Bean|beans]].{{Sfn|GWE|2021|qq=}} Some gwi-shin have supernatural foresight and can predict calamities, warn of impending doom, or reveal the location of lost objects.{{Sfn|GWE|2021|qq=}} However, they are generally depicted as being driven away by more powerful entities. In {{ill|Korean folklore|ja|朝鮮の伝説|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Korean folklore|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, figures like {{ill|Cheoyong|ko|처용|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cheoyong|qq=}}, who expelled evil spirits, appear on protective talismans (符籍) to ward off gwi-shin.{{Sfn|GWE|2021|qq=}} Additionally, stronger gwi-shin may prey upon weaker ones.{{Sfn|GWE|2021|qq=}} It is also believed that even divine spirits can turn into gwi-shin if they lose their place of worship or are forgotten by the living.{{Sfn|GWE|2021|qq=}} == References == {{reflist|qq=}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Global World Encyclopedia|publisher=Doosan <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Gwisin]][[es:Gwishin]][[fr:Gwishin]][[hu:Küsin]][[id:Gwishin]][[ja:鬼神]][[ko:귀신]][[zh:鬼神]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q5623686]] Wikidata name: Gwisin [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=There is an enwiki article but it seems horribly incomplete and possibly completely wrong scope[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 17:58, 13 February 2025 (UTC)|qq=}}
*
'''Kishin''' (鬼神, {{lang-en|''Demonic Gods'' or ''Spiritual Deities''|qq=}}) refers to supernatural entities in various cultural traditions. When pronounced as "kijin" or "kishin," it primarily denotes spirits or deities of the heavens and earth. When read as "onigami," it refers to unseen spirits or fierce and terrifying gods.{{Sfn|Kotobank|2021|qq=}} == China == {{Chinese cultural elements | pinyin = guǐshén | katakana = グイシェン | hiragana = きしん|qq=}} In {{ill|China|ja|中国|1=China|2=ja|3=中国|lt=China|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/China|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29520}}, the term "鬼神" (guǐshén) refers to two contrasting types of spirits: "鬼" (guǐ, ghosts) and "神" (shén, gods). * '''Gui (鬼)''' – Malevolent spirits that bring misfortune to the living, manifesting as {{ill|evil ghosts|ja|悪鬼|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/evil ghosts|1=evil ghosts|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492603}}. They are often the subject of exorcisms and prayers.{{Sfn|Makitani|2021|qq=}} Gui are the posthumous forms of the "魄" (pò), the yin-energy spirit of a living person, which governs the physical body. When a person dies, their pò remains in the earthly realm and transforms into a ghost. If they died a violent death or were left without a proper burial and veneration, they might become vengeful ghosts (幽鬼) and cause calamities.{{Sfn|Watanabe|2021|qq=}} * '''Shen (神)''' – Benevolent spirits that bring blessings to the living and are worshiped in {{ill|rituals|ja|祭祀|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rituals|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭|1=festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132241}}.{{Sfn|Makitani|2021|qq=}} Shen originate from the "魂" (hún), the yang-energy spirit of a living person, which governs the mind and ascends to the heavens after death, becoming a deity or divine spirit.{{Sfn|Makitani|2021|qq=}}{{Sfn|Watanabe|2021|qq=}} Additionally, the term "鬼神" can refer to the mysterious forces beyond human comprehension that create, change, and destroy all things in the universe.{{Sfn|Makitani|2021|qq=}} == Korea == {{Korean cultural elements | hangeul = 귀신 | katakana = クィシン|qq=}} In the [[:en:Folk religion|folk beliefs]] of the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|朝鮮半島|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Korean Peninsula|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Korean Peninsula|WD=Q483134}}, "gwi-shin" (귀신) refers to spirits that, unlike ancestral or guardian spirits, do not have a designated host and exist as independent entities.{{Sfn|GWE|2021|qq=}}{{Sfn|Soul Worship|2021|qq=}} This distinguishes them from spirits that reside within humans or natural elements, such as deities (신명, sinmyeong). However, over time, the distinction between these categories has blurred.{{Sfn|GWE|2021|qq=}}{{Sfn|Soul Worship|2021|qq=}} Gwi-shin are depicted as weak and ghostly beings, clad in fragile paper garments, with emaciated legs like glue-covered bones. They are powerless before divine beings and can shrink away when confronted by strong-willed humans.{{Sfn|GWE|2021|qq=}} Though typically invisible, they may be appeased with offerings of food. However, some gwi-shin are malevolent and bring misfortune.{{Sfn|GWE|2021|qq=}} During the daytime, gwi-shin wander through the air aimlessly, but at night, they seek damp places to rest.{{Sfn|GWE|2021|qq=}} They often reside in ancient trees, shadowy groves, underground spaces, or near wells and ponds. To keep them away, people scatter [[:en:Salt|salt]] or [[:en:Bean|beans]].{{Sfn|GWE|2021|qq=}} Some gwi-shin have supernatural foresight and can predict calamities, warn of impending doom, or reveal the location of lost objects.{{Sfn|GWE|2021|qq=}} However, they are generally depicted as being driven away by more powerful entities. In {{ill|Korean folklore|ja|朝鮮の伝説|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Korean folklore|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, figures like {{ill|Cheoyong|ko|처용|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cheoyong|qq=}}, who expelled evil spirits, appear on protective talismans (符籍) to ward off gwi-shin.{{Sfn|GWE|2021|qq=}} Additionally, stronger gwi-shin may prey upon weaker ones.{{Sfn|GWE|2021|qq=}} It is also believed that even divine spirits can turn into gwi-shin if they lose their place of worship or are forgotten by the living.{{Sfn|GWE|2021|qq=}} == References == {{reflist|qq=}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Global World Encyclopedia|publisher=Doosan Corporation|qq=}} * {{Cite web|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%AC%BC%E7%A5%9E-50543#E7.B2.BE.E9.81.B8.E7.89.88.20.E6.97.A5.E6.9C.AC.E5.9B.BD.E8.AA.9E.E5.A4.A7.E8.BE.9E.E5.85.B8 |title=鬼神 |publisher=Shogakukan |work=Nihon Kokugo Daijiten |date=2021-07-16 |language=ja|qq=}} * {{Cite web|url=https://ko.wikisource.org/wiki/%EA%B8%80%EB%A1%9C%EB%B2%8C_%EC%84%B8%EA%B3%84_%EB%8C%80%EB%B0%B1%EA%B3%BC%EC%82%AC%EC%A0%84/%EC%A2%85%EA%B5%90%C2%B7%EC%B2%A0%ED%95%99/%ED%95%9C%EA%B5%AD%EC%9D%98_%EC%A2%85%EA%B5%90/%EB%AF%BC_%EA%B0%84_%EC%8B%A0_%EC%95%99/%EC%88%AD_%EB%B0%B0#%EA%B7%80%EC%8B%A0 |title=귀신 (鬼神) |publisher=Global World Encyclopedia |date=2021-07-16 |language=ko|qq=}} == See Also == * [[:en:Arai Hakuseki|Arai Hakuseki]]'s {{ill|On Kishin|ja|鬼神論|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/On Kishin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Yamagata Bantō|Yamakata Bantō]]'s {{ill|Yume no Shiro|ja|夢の代|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yume no Shiro|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]]'s {{ill|Kishin Shinron|ja|鬼神新論|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishin Shinron|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

*
'''Kishin''' (鬼神, {{lang-en|''Demonic Gods'' or ''Spiritual Deities''|qq=}}) refers to supernatural entities in various cultural traditions. When pronounced as "kijin" or "kishin," it primarily denotes spirits or deities of the heavens and earth. When read as "onigami," it refers to unseen spirits or fierce and terrifying gods.{{Sfn|Kotobank|2021|qq=}} == China == {{Chinese cultural elements | pinyin = guǐshén | katakana = グイシェン | hiragana = きしん|qq=}} In {{ill|China|ja|中国|1=China|2=ja|3=中国|lt=China|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/China|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29520}}, the term "鬼神" (guǐshén) refers to two contrasting types of spirits: "鬼" (guǐ, ghosts) and "神" (shén, gods). * '''Gui (鬼)''' – Malevolent spirits that bring misfortune to the living, manifesting as {{ill|evil ghosts|ja|悪鬼|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/evil ghosts|1=evil ghosts|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492603}}. They are often the subject of exorcisms and prayers.{{Sfn|Makitani|2021|qq=}} Gui are the posthumous forms of the "魄" (pò), the yin-energy spirit of a living person, which governs the physical body. When a person dies, their pò remains in the earthly realm and transforms into a ghost. If they died a violent death or were left without a proper burial and veneration, they might become vengeful ghosts (幽鬼) and cause calamities.{{Sfn|Watanabe|2021|qq=}} * '''Shen (神)''' – Benevolent spirits that bring blessings to the living and are worshiped in {{ill|rituals|ja|祭祀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rituals|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭|1=festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132241}}.{{Sfn|Makitani|2021|qq=}} Shen originate from the "魂" (hún), the yang-energy spirit of a living person, which governs the mind and ascends to the heavens after death, becoming a deity or divine spirit.{{Sfn|Makitani|2021|qq=}}{{Sfn|Watanabe|2021|qq=}} Additionally, the term "鬼神" can refer to the mysterious forces beyond human comprehension that create, change, and destroy all things in the universe.{{Sfn|Makitani|2021|qq=}} == Korea == {{Korean cultural elements | hangeul = 귀신 | katakana = クィシン|qq=}} In the [[:en:Folk religion|folk beliefs]] of the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|朝鮮半島|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Korean Peninsula|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Korean Peninsula|WD=Q483134}}, "gwi-shin" (귀신) refers to spirits that, unlike ancestral or guardian spirits, do not have a designated host and exist as independent entities.{{Sfn|GWE|2021|qq=}}{{Sfn|Soul Worship|2021|qq=}} This distinguishes them from spirits that reside within humans or natural elements, such as deities (신명, sinmyeong). However, over time, the distinction between these categories has blurred.{{Sfn|GWE|2021|qq=}}{{Sfn|Soul Worship|2021|qq=}} Gwi-shin are depicted as weak and ghostly beings, clad in fragile paper garments, with emaciated legs like glue-covered bones. They are powerless before divine beings and can shrink away when confronted by strong-willed humans.{{Sfn|GWE|2021|qq=}} Though typically invisible, they may be appeased with offerings of food. However, some gwi-shin are malevolent and bring misfortune.{{Sfn|GWE|2021|qq=}} During the daytime, gwi-shin wander through the air aimlessly, but at night, they seek damp places to rest.{{Sfn|GWE|2021|qq=}} They often reside in ancient trees, shadowy groves, underground spaces, or near wells and ponds. To keep them away, people scatter [[:en:Salt|salt]] or [[:en:Bean|beans]].{{Sfn|GWE|2021|qq=}} Some gwi-shin have supernatural foresight and can predict calamities, warn of impending doom, or reveal the location of lost objects.{{Sfn|GWE|2021|qq=}} However, they are generally depicted as being driven away by more powerful entities. In {{ill|Korean folklore|ja|朝鮮の伝説|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Korean folklore|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, figures like {{ill|Cheoyong|ko|처용|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cheoyong|qq=}}, who expelled evil spirits, appear on protective talismans (符籍) to ward off gwi-shin.{{Sfn|GWE|2021|qq=}} Additionally, stronger gwi-shin may prey upon weaker ones.{{Sfn|GWE|2021|qq=}} It is also believed that even divine spirits can turn into gwi-shin if they lose their place of worship or are forgotten by the living.{{Sfn|GWE|2021|qq=}} == References == {{reflist|qq=}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Global World Encyclopedia|publisher=Doosan Corporation|qq=}} * {{Cite web|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%AC%BC%E7%A5%9E-50543#E7.B2.BE.E9.81.B8.E7.89.88.20.E6.97.A5.E6.9C.AC.E5.9B.BD.E8.AA.9E.E5.A4.A7.E8.BE.9E.E5.85.B8 |title=鬼神 |publisher=Shogakukan |work=Nihon Kokugo Daijiten |date=2021-07-16 |language=ja|qq=}} * {{Cite web|url=https://ko.wikisource.org/wiki/%EA%B8%80%EB%A1%9C%EB%B2%8C_%EC%84%B8%EA%B3%84_%EB%8C%80%EB%B0%B1%EA%B3%BC%EC%82%AC%EC%A0%84/%EC%A2%85%EA%B5%90%C2%B7%EC%B2%A0%ED%95%99/%ED%95%9C%EA%B5%AD%EC%9D%98_%EC%A2%85%EA%B5%90/%EB%AF%BC_%EA%B0%84_%EC%8B%A0_%EC%95%99/%EC%88%AD_%EB%B0%B0#%EA%B7%80%EC%8B%A0 |title=귀신 (鬼神) |publisher=Global World Encyclopedia |date=2021-07-16 |language=ko|qq=}} == See Also == * [[:en:Arai Hakuseki|Arai Hakuseki]]'s {{ill|On Kishin|ja|鬼神論|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/On Kishin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Yamagata Bantō|Yamakata Bantō]]'s {{ill|Yume no Shiro|ja|夢の代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yume no Shiro|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]]'s {{ill|Kishin Shinron|ja|鬼神新論|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishin Shinron|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Gwisin]][[es:Gwishin]][[fr:Gwishin]][[hu:Küsin]][[id:Gwishin]][[ja:鬼神]][[ko:귀신]][[zh:鬼神]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q5623686]] Wikidata name: Gwisin [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=There is an enwiki article but it seems horribly incomplete and possibly completely wrong scope[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 17:58, 13 February 2025 (UTC)|qq=}}
*
'''Kishin''' (鬼神, {{lang-en|''Demonic Gods'' or ''Spiritual Deities''|qq=}}) refers to supernatural entities in various cultural traditions. When pronounced as "kijin" or "kishin," it primarily denotes spirits or deities of the heavens and earth. When read as "onigami," it refers to unseen spirits or fierce and terrifying gods.{{Sfn|Kotobank|2021|qq=}} == China == {{Chinese cultural elements | pinyin = guǐshén | katakana = グイシェン | hiragana = きしん|qq=}} In {{ill|China|ja|中国|1=China|2=ja|3=中国|lt=China|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/China|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29520}}, the term "鬼神" (guǐshén) refers to two contrasting types of spirits: "鬼" (guǐ, ghosts) and "神" (shén, gods). * '''Gui (鬼)''' – Malevolent spirits that bring misfortune to the living, manifesting as {{ill|evil ghosts|ja|悪鬼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/evil ghosts|1=evil ghosts|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492603}}. They are often the subject of exorcisms and prayers.{{Sfn|Makitani|2021|qq=}} Gui are the posthumous forms of the "魄" (pò), the yin-energy spirit of a living person, which governs the physical body. When a person dies, their pò remains in the earthly realm and transforms into a ghost. If they died a violent death or were left without a proper burial and veneration, they might become vengeful ghosts (幽鬼) and cause calamities.{{Sfn|Watanabe|2021|qq=}} * '''Shen (神)''' – Benevolent spirits that bring blessings to the living and are worshiped in {{ill|rituals|ja|祭祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rituals|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭|1=festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132241}}.{{Sfn|Makitani|2021|qq=}} Shen originate from the "魂" (hún), the yang-energy spirit of a living person, which governs the mind and ascends to the heavens after death, becoming a deity or divine spirit.{{Sfn|Makitani|2021|qq=}}{{Sfn|Watanabe|2021|qq=}} Additionally, the term "鬼神" can refer to the mysterious forces beyond human comprehension that create, change, and destroy all things in the universe.{{Sfn|Makitani|2021|qq=}} == Korea == {{Korean cultural elements | hangeul = 귀신 | katakana = クィシン|qq=}} In the [[:en:Folk religion|folk beliefs]] of the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|朝鮮半島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Korean Peninsula|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Korean Peninsula|WD=Q483134}}, "gwi-shin" (귀신) refers to spirits that, unlike ancestral or guardian spirits, do not have a designated host and exist as independent entities.{{Sfn|GWE|2021|qq=}}{{Sfn|Soul Worship|2021|qq=}} This distinguishes them from spirits that reside within humans or natural elements, such as deities (신명, sinmyeong). However, over time, the distinction between these categories has blurred.{{Sfn|GWE|2021|qq=}}{{Sfn|Soul Worship|2021|qq=}} Gwi-shin are depicted as weak and ghostly beings, clad in fragile paper garments, with emaciated legs like glue-covered bones. They are powerless before divine beings and can shrink away when confronted by strong-willed humans.{{Sfn|GWE|2021|qq=}} Though typically invisible, they may be appeased with offerings of food. However, some gwi-shin are malevolent and bring misfortune.{{Sfn|GWE|2021|qq=}} During the daytime, gwi-shin wander through the air aimlessly, but at night, they seek damp places to rest.{{Sfn|GWE|2021|qq=}} They often reside in ancient trees, shadowy groves, underground spaces, or near wells and ponds. To keep them away, people scatter [[:en:Salt|salt]] or [[:en:Bean|beans]].{{Sfn|GWE|2021|qq=}} Some gwi-shin have supernatural foresight and can predict calamities, warn of impending doom, or reveal the location of lost objects.{{Sfn|GWE|2021|qq=}} However, they are generally depicted as being driven away by more powerful entities. In {{ill|Korean folklore|ja|朝鮮の伝説|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Korean folklore|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, figures like {{ill|Cheoyong|ko|처용|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cheoyong|qq=}}, who expelled evil spirits, appear on protective talismans (符籍) to ward off gwi-shin.{{Sfn|GWE|2021|qq=}} Additionally, stronger gwi-shin may prey upon weaker ones.{{Sfn|GWE|2021|qq=}} It is also believed that even divine spirits can turn into gwi-shin if they lose their place of worship or are forgotten by the living.{{Sfn|GWE|2021|qq=}} == References == {{reflist|qq=}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Global World Encyclopedia|publisher=Doosan Corporation|qq=}} * {{Cite web|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%AC%BC%E7%A5%9E-50543#E7.B2.BE.E9.81.B8.E7.89.88.20.E6.97.A5.E6.9C.AC.E5.9B.BD.E8.AA.9E.E5.A4.A7.E8.BE.9E.E5.85.B8 |title=鬼神 |publisher=Shogakukan |work=Nihon Kokugo Daijiten |date=2021-07-16 |language=ja|qq=}} * {{Cite web|url=https://ko.wikisource.org/wiki/%EA%B8%80%EB%A1%9C%EB%B2%8C_%EC%84%B8%EA%B3%84_%EB%8C%80%EB%B0%B1%EA%B3%BC%EC%82%AC%EC%A0%84/%EC%A2%85%EA%B5%90%C2%B7%EC%B2%A0%ED%95%99/%ED%95%9C%EA%B5%AD%EC%9D%98_%EC%A2%85%EA%B5%90/%EB%AF%BC_%EA%B0%84_%EC%8B%A0_%EC%95%99/%EC%88%AD_%EB%B0%B0#%EA%B7%80%EC%8B%A0 |title=귀신 (鬼神) |publisher=Global World Encyclopedia |date=2021-07-16 |language=ko|qq=}} == See Also == * [[:en:Arai Hakuseki|Arai Hakuseki]]'s {{ill|On Kishin|ja|鬼神論|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/On Kishin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Yamagata Bantō|Yamakata Bantō]]'s {{ill|Yume no Shiro|ja|夢の代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yume no Shiro|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]]'s {{ill|Kishin Shinron|ja|鬼神新論|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishin Shinron|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
{{translated page|ja|鬼神|version=102380970|comment=Imported full ja history}}

However, some gwi-shin are malevolent and bring misfortune.{{Sfn|GWE|2021|qq=}} During the daytime, gwi-shin wander through the air aimlessly, but at night, they seek damp places to rest.{{Sfn|GWE|2021|qq=}} They often reside in ancient trees, shadowy groves, underground spaces, or near wells and ponds. To keep them away, people scatter [[:en:Salt|salt]] or [[:en:Bean|beans]].{{Sfn|GWE|2021|qq=}} Some gwi-shin have supernatural foresight and can predict calamities, warn of impending doom, or reveal the location of lost objects.{{Sfn|GWE|2021|qq=}} However, they are generally depicted as being driven away by more powerful entities. In {{ill|Korean folklore|ja|朝鮮の伝説|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Korean folklore|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, figures like {{ill|Cheoyong|ko|처용|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cheoyong|qq=}}, who expelled evil spirits, appear on protective talismans (符籍) to ward off gwi-shin.{{Sfn|GWE|2021|qq=}} Additionally, stronger gwi-shin may prey upon weaker ones.{{Sfn|GWE|2021|qq=}} It is also believed that even divine spirits can turn into gwi-shin if they lose their place of worship or are forgotten by the living.{{Sfn|GWE|2021|qq=}} == References == {{reflist|qq=}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Global World Encyclopedia|publisher=Doosan Corporation|qq=}} * {{Cite web|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%AC%BC%E7%A5%9E-50543#E7.B2.BE.E9.81.B8.E7.89.88.20.E6.97.A5.E6.9C.AC.E5.9B.BD.E8.AA.9E.E5.A4.A7.E8.BE.9E.E5.85.B8 |title=鬼神 |publisher=Shogakukan |work=Nihon Kokugo Daijiten |date=2021-07-16 |language=ja|qq=}} * {{Cite web|url=https://ko.wikisource.org/wiki/%EA%B8%80%EB%A1%9C%EB%B2%8C_%EC%84%B8%EA%B3%84_%EB%8C%80%EB%B0%B1%EA%B3%BC%EC%82%AC%EC%A0%84/%EC%A2%85%EA%B5%90%C2%B7%EC%B2%A0%ED%95%99/%ED%95%9C%EA%B5%AD%EC%9D%98_%EC%A2%85%EA%B5%90/%EB%AF%BC_%EA%B0%84_%EC%8B%A0_%EC%95%99/%EC%88%AD_%EB%B0%B0#%EA%B7%80%EC%8B%A0 |title=귀신 (鬼神) |publisher=Global World Encyclopedia |date=2021-07-16 |language=ko|qq=}} == See Also == * [[:en:Arai Hakuseki|Arai Hakuseki]]'s {{ill|On Kishin|ja|鬼神論|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/On Kishin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Yamagata Bantō|Yamakata Bantō]]'s {{ill|Yume no Shiro|ja|夢の代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yume no Shiro|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]]'s {{ill|Kishin Shinron|ja|鬼神新論|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishin Shinron|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Gwisin]][[es:Gwishin]][[fr:Gwishin]][[hu:Küsin]][[id:Gwishin]][[ja:鬼神]][[ko:귀신]][[zh:鬼神]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q5623686]] Wikidata name: Gwisin [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=There is an enwiki article but it seems horribly incomplete and possibly completely wrong scope[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 17:58, 13 February 2025 (UTC)|qq=}}
*
'''Kishin''' (鬼神, {{lang-en|''Demonic Gods'' or ''Spiritual Deities''|qq=}}) refers to supernatural entities in various cultural traditions. When pronounced as "kijin" or "kishin," it primarily denotes spirits or deities of the heavens and earth. When read as "onigami," it refers to unseen spirits or fierce and terrifying gods.{{Sfn|Kotobank|2021|qq=}} == China == {{Chinese cultural elements | pinyin = guǐshén | katakana = グイシェン | hiragana = きしん|qq=}} In {{ill|China|ja|中国|1=China|2=ja|3=中国|lt=China|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/China|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29520}}, the term "鬼神" (guǐshén) refers to two contrasting types of spirits: "鬼" (guǐ, ghosts) and "神" (shén, gods). * '''Gui (鬼)''' – Malevolent spirits that bring misfortune to the living, manifesting as {{ill|evil ghosts|ja|悪鬼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/evil ghosts|1=evil ghosts|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492603}}. They are often the subject of exorcisms and prayers.{{Sfn|Makitani|2021|qq=}} Gui are the posthumous forms of the "魄" (pò), the yin-energy spirit of a living person, which governs the physical body. When a person dies, their pò remains in the earthly realm and transforms into a ghost. If they died a violent death or were left without a proper burial and veneration, they might become vengeful ghosts (幽鬼) and cause calamities.{{Sfn|Watanabe|2021|qq=}} * '''Shen (神)''' – Benevolent spirits that bring blessings to the living and are worshiped in {{ill|rituals|ja|祭祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rituals|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭|1=festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132241}}.{{Sfn|Makitani|2021|qq=}} Shen originate from the "魂" (hún), the yang-energy spirit of a living person, which governs the mind and ascends to the heavens after death, becoming a deity or divine spirit.{{Sfn|Makitani|2021|qq=}}{{Sfn|Watanabe|2021|qq=}} Additionally, the term "鬼神" can refer to the mysterious forces beyond human comprehension that create, change, and destroy all things in the universe.{{Sfn|Makitani|2021|qq=}} == Korea == {{Korean cultural elements | hangeul = 귀신 | katakana = クィシン|qq=}} In the [[:en:Folk religion|folk beliefs]] of the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|朝鮮半島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Korean Peninsula|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Korean Peninsula|WD=Q483134}}, "gwi-shin" (귀신) refers to spirits that, unlike ancestral or guardian spirits, do not have a designated host and exist as independent entities.{{Sfn|GWE|2021|qq=}}{{Sfn|Soul Worship|2021|qq=}} This distinguishes them from spirits that reside within humans or natural elements, such as deities (신명, sinmyeong). However, over time, the distinction between these categories has blurred.{{Sfn|GWE|2021|qq=}}{{Sfn|Soul Worship|2021|qq=}} Gwi-shin are depicted as weak and ghostly beings, clad in fragile paper garments, with emaciated legs like glue-covered bones. They are powerless before divine beings and can shrink away when confronted by strong-willed humans.{{Sfn|GWE|2021|qq=}} Though typically invisible, they may be appeased with offerings of food. However, some gwi-shin are malevolent and bring misfortune.{{Sfn|GWE|2021|qq=}} During the daytime, gwi-shin wander through the air aimlessly, but at night, they seek damp places to rest.{{Sfn|GWE|2021|qq=}} They often reside in ancient trees, shadowy groves, underground spaces, or near wells and ponds. To keep them away, people scatter [[:en:Salt|salt]] or [[:en:Bean|beans]].{{Sfn|GWE|2021|qq=}} Some gwi-shin have supernatural foresight and can predict calamities, warn of impending doom, or reveal the location of lost objects.{{Sfn|GWE|2021|qq=}} However, they are generally depicted as being driven away by more powerful entities. In {{ill|Korean folklore|ja|朝鮮の伝説|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Korean folklore|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, figures like {{ill|Cheoyong|ko|처용|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cheoyong|qq=}}, who expelled evil spirits, appear on protective talismans (符籍) to ward off gwi-shin.{{Sfn|GWE|2021|qq=}} Additionally, stronger gwi-shin may prey upon weaker ones.{{Sfn|GWE|2021|qq=}} It is also believed that even divine spirits can turn into gwi-shin if they lose their place of worship or are forgotten by the living.{{Sfn|GWE|2021|qq=}} == References == {{reflist|qq=}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Global World Encyclopedia|publisher=Doosan Corporation|qq=}} * {{Cite web|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%AC%BC%E7%A5%9E-50543#E7.B2.BE.E9.81.B8.E7.89.88.20.E6.97.A5.E6.9C.AC.E5.9B.BD.E8.AA.9E.E5.A4.A7.E8.BE.9E.E5.85.B8 |title=鬼神 |publisher=Shogakukan |work=Nihon Kokugo Daijiten |date=2021-07-16 |language=ja|qq=}} * {{Cite web|url=https://ko.wikisource.org/wiki/%EA%B8%80%EB%A1%9C%EB%B2%8C_%EC%84%B8%EA%B3%84_%EB%8C%80%EB%B0%B1%EA%B3%BC%EC%82%AC%EC%A0%84/%EC%A2%85%EA%B5%90%C2%B7%EC%B2%A0%ED%95%99/%ED%95%9C%EA%B5%AD%EC%9D%98_%EC%A2%85%EA%B5%90/%EB%AF%BC_%EA%B0%84_%EC%8B%A0_%EC%95%99/%EC%88%AD_%EB%B0%B0#%EA%B7%80%EC%8B%A0 |title=귀신 (鬼神) |publisher=Global World Encyclopedia |date=2021-07-16 |language=ko|qq=}} == See Also == * [[:en:Arai Hakuseki|Arai Hakuseki]]'s {{ill|On Kishin|ja|鬼神論|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/On Kishin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Yamagata Bantō|Yamakata Bantō]]'s {{ill|Yume no Shiro|ja|夢の代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yume no Shiro|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]]'s {{ill|Kishin Shinron|ja|鬼神新論|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishin Shinron|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
